items.headline: Kenyan runner breaks two-hour marathon barrier in official race
items.body: Sawe finished the London Marathon in 1:59:30 on April 26, becoming the first person to break two hours in a sanctioned competition. The barrier was considered one of sport's most symbolic thresholds affecting elite distance running standards worldwide.

items.headline: Elon Musk breach of trust trial against Sam Altman begins in federal court
items.body: A nine-person jury was seated April 27 in Oakland to hear Musk's claims that Altman breached charitable obligations when converting OpenAI to for-profit structure. The case could reshape legal boundaries for nonprofit-to-profit conversions in the technology sector.

items.headline: Iran offers ceasefire proposal separating Hormuz reopening from nuclear talks
items.body: Iran submitted a new proposal April 26 through Pakistani mediators to end the Strait of Hormuz blockade and extend the ceasefire before addressing nuclear issues. The sequencing reverses prior U.S. insistence on simultaneous resolution of both issues.

items.headline: India and Russia operationalize mutual troop deployment pact allowing 3,000 soldiers on each territory
items.body: Russia published details in April 2026 of a January pact permitting each country to station 3,000 troops, five warships, and ten aircraft on the other's soil during peace or war. India has not granted similar permanent stationing rights to any other country.
